title: Workhorse Group Full Year 2024 Results on Seeking Alpha
text: Workhorse Group reported full year 2024 results showing sales declined to $6.6 million from $13.1 million year-over-year, primarily due to lower W4 CC truck sales partially offset by increased W56 sales and service revenue. Net loss of $101.8 million in 2024 improved from $123.9 million in 2023. The company secured regulatory approval in Canada for W4 CC and W56 models with plans to deploy demo trucks in April 2025. Workhorse launched the W56 208-inch wheelbase model with 1,200 cubic feet cargo capacity and received an order for 13 extended wheelbase vehicles. Recent accomplishments include a GSA contract, sales to the City of Tacoma, and California state contracts. The Stables by Workhorse initiative delivered nearly 154,000 packages during 2024 peak season. As of December 31, 2024, Workhorse held $4.6 million in cash with $8.2 million in working capital.
